summary: High‑energy second set with stellar China>Rider and tight jams
blurb: The show shines in the second set, where a fiery China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue and a seamless Eyes Of The World → Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance flow dominate. Jerry recovers from an early lyrical slip to deliver a possessed guitar on Rider, while the drum duo crafts a memorable jam. Overall a solid performance worth hearing for the standout moments.
review: December 27, 1979 at Oakland Auditorium Arena captures the Dead in a late‑year surge of energy. The first set meanders a bit—Jerry even flubs the opening lyric to China Cat Sunflower—but the band quickly finds its groove. By the time they launch into the classic China Cat → I Know You Rider transition, Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Bob Weir rides “on another planet,” delivering one of his most inspired rhythm parts of the era. The set builds through Samson & Delilah into a soaring Eyes Of The World that segues into Lost Sailor and Saint Of Circumstance, keeping the momentum alive. A highlight comes midway with an inventive drum jam that showcases both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann in top form, leading into Space and the uplifting Black Peter → Sugar Magnolia finale. The encore rounds out the night with U.S. Blues and a brief Brokedown Palace tease, though one audience tape cuts off the ending. For fans seeking the clearest window onto this performance, the audience recording gd79-12-27.rolfe.vernon.15081.sbeok.shnf offers excellent ambience and captures the show’s high‑octane second set with minimal flaws.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are positive, highlighting the China>Rider segue, Eyes‑World sequence and drum jam; two are negative about cut‑offs or mislabeling, and one mixed notes uneven first set. The overall sentiment leans positive but is not uniformly glowing, warranting a moderate +0.8 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Best‑sounding audience tape; clear, well‑balanced and captures the high‑energy second set with minimal imperfections.

key_highlights: Fire‑brand China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Seamless Eyes Of The World → Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance flow
Engaging drum jam during the Space segment
Strong vocal and guitar work from Jerry after early lyrical slip

band_performance.Jerry: Starts with a lyrical flub on China Cat but quickly recovers, delivering a possessed guitar solo on I Know You Rider and driving the jam sections with intensity.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation; noted as slightly boomy due to hall acoustics but otherwise steady throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Rocks an “on another planet” rhythm performance, especially evident during the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Brent: Offers reliable keyboard textures that support the jams and tease the Brokedown Palace fragment.
band_performance.Mickey: Partners with Bill in a standout drum jam that adds excitement to the Space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Works alongside Mickey to create an inventive rhythmic landscape, highlighted by the mid‑set drum excursion.
